title: Devol Robots Inc.
tagline: AI-driven autonomous robot with human-like touch and learning capabilities
description: Devol Robots Inc. is a robotics company building AI-driven autonomous robots focused on manipulation, force control, and adaptive motion. Its public site highlights demonstrations in pick-and-place, manufacturing tasks, pin assembly, snap-fit assembly, impedance control, touch-safe robotics, gravity compensation, and smart path change.
challenge: Devol Robots is focused on robotic systems that can better handle contact-rich, real-world tasks where touch, force, adaptation, and safety matter.
challengePoints: Robots need to understand contact and adapt in real time
Assembly and manipulation tasks require force sensitivity and alignment
Human-safe automation depends on responsive touch-aware behavior
Industrial environments need robots that can adjust when paths are blocked or conditions change
ourRole: SF Playground featured Devol Robots Inc. in Pitch Playoffs 002 as a robotics startup building autonomous systems for real-world manipulation and manufacturing tasks.
rolePoints: Featured the company in Pitch Playoffs 002
Highlighted a robotics startup focused on adaptive, touch-aware automation
experience: Devol’s public site presents the company through task demos rather than long product copy, showing examples like daily-object pick and place, manufacturing pick and place, force-control pin assembly, snap-fit assembly, impedance control, touch-safe stopping behavior, gravity compensation, and automatic path changes around obstacles.
impact: Devol’s public materials emphasize robotic capabilities around learning, force control, safety, and adaptive task execution. The company’s site suggests a focus on making robots better at handling physical interaction rather than only repeating rigid motions.
impactPoints: Public demos include pick-and-place for daily objects
Public demos include pick-and-place for manufacturing
Public demos include force-control pin assembly
Public demos include snap-fit assembly
Public demos include impedance control
Public demos include touch-safe stopping behavior
Public demos include gravity compensation
Public demos include smart path change around obstacles
Site says the robots have human-like touch and learning capabilities
whyItMatters: Devol Robots stands out because it is centered on physical intelligence: robots that can feel contact, adapt motion, and operate more safely in real environments.

title: Orboh
description: Orboh is building humanoid robotics for construction, with public materials highlighting use cases such as scaffolding assembly, material transport, and inspection work.
challenge: Construction sites still depend heavily on manual labor for repetitive, physically demanding, and inspection-heavy workflows that are difficult to automate with conventional systems.
challengePoints: Construction work includes repetitive and physically demanding tasks
Sites need better solutions for scaffolding assembly and material transport
Inspection work in construction remains labor intensive
Robotics for construction has to operate in dynamic, real-world jobsite conditions
ourRole: SF Playground featured Orboh as a participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002.
rolePoints: Participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002
Highlighted a robotics company focused on construction
experience: Orboh’s site presents a focused construction robotics thesis rather than a broad automation platform, centering on humanoid systems for jobsite tasks.
impact: The company’s public positioning emphasizes automation for core construction workflows where labor intensity and jobsite complexity create bottlenecks.
impactPoints: Focused on humanoid robotics for construction
Highlights scaffolding assembly as a use case
Highlights material transport as a use case
Highlights inspection work as a use case
whyItMatters: Orboh stands out for targeting one of the hardest physical environments to automate: real construction sites.

title: Petpin
description: Petpin is building a premium pet wearable designed to help owners stay connected to their pets. Its public materials describe features such as live pet POV video, GPS tracking, and AI-driven insights.
challenge: Pet owners have limited real-time visibility into what their pets are doing, where they are, and how they may be feeling throughout the day.
challengePoints: Pet owners often lack real-time visibility into their pet’s daily experience
Tracking location and activity can be fragmented across different tools
Understanding pet behavior and signals remains difficult for many owners
ourRole: SF Playground featured Petpin as a participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002.
rolePoints: Participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002
Physical product showcased at the event
experience: Petpin’s public site positions the company around a polished consumer hardware experience for pet owners, centered on a connected wearable device.
impact: Petpin’s public materials emphasize a more immersive and intelligent form of pet monitoring through wearable hardware and AI-powered features.
impactPoints: Premium pet wearable device
Public materials reference live pet POV video
Public materials reference GPS tracking
Public materials reference AI-driven insights
whyItMatters: Petpin shows how Pitch Playoffs can highlight consumer hardware startups that combine emotion, utility, and AI in a tangible product.

title: Deus Robotics
description: Deus Robotics builds an automation platform that combines AI, robotics, and software to reduce manual work and improve operational efficiency across industries.
challenge: Businesses adopting robotics often face fragmented systems, vendor lock-in, and operational complexity when trying to scale automation across facilities and workflows.
challengePoints: Robotic systems are often fragmented across vendors
Scaling automation requires orchestration, support, and integration
Operations teams need better ways to reduce manual work across facilities
ourRole: SF Playground featured Deus Robotics as a participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002.
rolePoints: Participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002
Physical product showcased at the event
experience: Deus Robotics publicly positions itself around unifying robots from different vendors into a coordinated automation system, supported by software, AI automation, and on-site onboarding.
impact: The company’s public materials emphasize orchestration and end-to-end automation, with solutions aimed at fulfillment, retail, warehouse and logistics, service spaces, and manufacturing.
impactPoints: Combines AI, robotics, and software in one system
Focuses on reducing manual work and improving operational efficiency
Public materials reference vendor-agnostic robot management
Targets fulfillment, retail, warehouse and logistics, service spaces, and manufacturing
whyItMatters: Deus Robotics adds credibility to the lineup by representing a company working on the software and orchestration layer needed to scale robotics adoption.

title: Occurrence AB
tagline: The better way to monitor your operations
description: Occurrence AB is a Physical AI company that turns existing camera streams into real-time, privacy-preserving operational insight for manufacturing and other industrial environments.
challenge: Occurrence says that while machines are connected, frontline work often is not. That gap can hide efficiency loss, increase safety risk, and slow human-robot collaboration, especially in industrial settings.
challengePoints: Frontline work is often not digitally connected even when machines are
Limited real-time visibility can hide efficiency loss and safety risk
Human-robot collaboration is harder when live human activity is not captured and shared
Industrial teams need privacy-preserving monitoring without relying on stored video
ourRole: SF Playground featured Occurrence Technologies in Pitch Playoffs 002 as a startup building Physical AI infrastructure for safer and smarter operations.
rolePoints: Featured the company in Pitch Playoffs 002
Highlighted a Physical AI startup working on manufacturing and operations visibility
Included a team bringing a portable demonstrator for live showcase
experience: Occurrence presents itself as a human-centric Physical AI platform for industry. Its site highlights capabilities like real-time positioning, full-body 3D pose estimation without wearables, workflow analytics, geofencing, alerts, robotic integration, and deployment using existing camera systems.
impact: Occurrence’s public materials emphasize safety, efficiency, and privacy-preserving operational insight. The company says its platform supports use cases like industrial ergonomics, workflow analytics, and human-robot collaboration while avoiding storage of video data.
impactPoints: Uses existing camera streams as system input
Provides real-time monitoring and analytics
Supports geofencing and automated alerts
Processes anonymised positional data instead of storing video
Designed for deployment on-prem or in cloud environments
Supports integration via MQTT and REST API
Targets industries including manufacturing, logistics, smart cities, retail, and healthcare
Lists Sting, NVIDIA, AWS, EIT, DeepTech Alliance, SII, and TUM under partners and programmes
Stockholm, Sweden and Munich, Germany locations are listed on the site
whyItMatters: Occurrence stands out because it is not just building another vision system. It is positioning camera-based Physical AI as a privacy-conscious operational layer that can improve safety, throughput, and human-robot coordination across real industrial environments.

challenge: Hardware development in robotics is often slow and complex, making it difficult for teams to iterate quickly or adapt solutions across different applications.
challengePoints: Robotics hardware development is often complex and time consuming

impactPoints: Designing plug-and-play hardware and software modules

title: Novos Power
tagline: Grid-ready for AI data centers and beyond
description: Novos Power is building power infrastructure for AI data centers using its Variable Airgap Solid State Transformer technology, positioned around speed, reliability, scalability, and direct utility-feed interconnects.
challenge: AI data centers face megawatt-scale, millisecond-level power swings that strain legacy power delivery systems and create infrastructure bottlenecks.
challengePoints: AI data centers face megawatt-scale power swings
Legacy 480V transformer architectures create constraints
Power infrastructure needs faster dynamic response and higher density delivery
ourRole: SF Playground featured Novos Power as a participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002.
rolePoints: Participating startup in Pitch Playoffs 002
Physical product showcased at the event
experience: Novos Power’s public site frames the company around grid-ready infrastructure for AI data centers, grounded in DOE- and NASA-funded research.
impact: The company’s public materials emphasize scalable, high-density power delivery and direct 13 to 48 kV utility-feed interconnects for next-generation compute infrastructure.
impactPoints: Targets AI data center power infrastructure
Built around Variable Airgap Solid State Transformer technology
Public site references DOE- and NASA-funded research
Public site references 300+ peer-reviewed publications and 30+ issued patents behind the foundation
whyItMatters: Novos Power illustrates how the event can attract infrastructure startups working on a critical bottleneck for AI growth: power delivery.

title: 1Robot
